methods.method_name: Static GPS Data Collection and Processing
methods.description: This method describes the collection and processing of GPS data to measure shoreline height using Trimble dual-frequency receivers. The procedure involves collecting data during two survey periods, processing baselines, and obtaining fixed solutions for precise coordinates.

steps.description: GPS data that measure shoreline height were collected during two survey periods in 2015 and 2016 using two Trimble dual-frequency receivers: 5700 and NetR9 with Zephyr and Zephyr Geodetic antennas.
steps.searchable_summary: collect GPS data using Trimble dual-frequency receivers

steps.description: Each GPS point was occupied for at least 10 min, following a rapid static method.
steps.searchable_summary: occupy each GPS point for 10 minutes using rapid static method

steps.description: To obtain precise coordinates for each point, we followed the GPS differential procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: apply GPS differential procedure for precise coordinates

steps.description: We processed baselines and obtained fixed solutions for 64 GPS points; when two baselines were processed for the same point, we adjusted both results for a robust solution.
steps.searchable_summary: process baselines and obtain fixed solutions for GPS points

steps.description: The mean difference between the elevations in the DEM and the GPS sites was 0.27 ± 1.07 m. Of the 64 GPS sites, 48 (75%) agreed within 1 m with the DEM.
steps.searchable_summary: validate GPS data against DEM elevations

steps.description: Thus, we attributed a total uncertainty for each GPS position of ±3 m.
